reviews.review_title: I didn't expect too much but wow!!!
reviews.review_description: I really like this retro style wave shooter with medieval fantasy twist and it’s honestly super chill. The shooting is just smooth enough that you can zone out and relax. I’ve been laying in bed, blasting away enemies, and it’s weirdly calming. If you’re into a laid-back shooter vibe, this one’s pretty great.

reviews.review_title: A fun romp through the lands of gods!
reviews.review_description:
This game is honestly awesome and surprisingly challenging in parts. It has a wicked soundtrack, lots of depth, and plenty of customization. You move a lot more than you’d expect, constantly dodging projectiles and enemy attacks, which keeps the action intense.

You won’t finish it in one sitting since there are many worlds, challenges, and items to unlock. The epic metal soundtrack fits the action perfectly! The settings are a real visual delight too and look great as you blast your way through each stage. Be ready though, this game will definitely give your controller a workout!
Weapons work well, the graphics are nice and it has a lot of bosses to take down

I’m typically a open world shooter style gamer but I really enjoyed this game I like how the weapon’s feel when they shoot and the trails make it so comfortable to point and shoot the enemies it took me a second to realize I needed to dodge some of the enemy attacks I skipped the tutorial 😇 and went straight to slaying  I’m sure the developers will have some really fire games in the future too

Oh my goodness, can I just say this game is freaking amazing. Like, the soundtrack is awesome, just like Simon, aka my boyfriend said, but I was like, OK, this is good. You know, this level right here is slow. I'm good. I'm doing it, you know? And then all of a sudden the music picks up and it's just like throwing enemies at me one by one and I'm like, Oh my God, like holy crap, this is freaking amazing anyway. Yeah. This game is awesome.
Best railshooter for the Quest

Elysium Trials is a fast-paced on-rails shooter that delivers a solid challenge and a seriously outstanding soundtrack. The fantasy environments are imaginative and varied, and dodging attacks with crossbow in hand feels engaging, especially during the intense boss fights. 

However, after extended play, the enemy types and core mechanics start to feel a bit repetitive, with not much to mix things up across runs. While the graphics and immersion are enjoyable, it’s not the most innovative of VR shooters by 2025 standards.

Overall, if you enjoy skill-focused VR action and are looking for an adrenaline rush set to epic rock and metal music, this is an entertaining pick!

developer_response.body: Hey Adin, glad you enjoyed our game. Sounds like you might be at a skill level ready for champion mode difficulty, where there are 4 more unique enemy types added to the mix, and new mechanics on bosses!

reviews.review_title: this game is gorgeous! i will explain!
reviews.review_description: this game got me immersed from the very beginning, i literally got chills and forgot about the real world for hours, from the built in training most games don't do nowadays to the game play itself! i thought finally i got that same feeling i got when i first got my quest 1, and played the game space trainer, which is what should've been what elysium trials is, yes its challenging thats the beauty of it, it has so many different elements in this game that makes it so special, I can't sleep until I finish this game its become personal, I can tell and feel the team that made this game really put their hearts into it, at the end of the day i felt like that guy from the movie avatar when he got up from the bed after he got lost in the woods and the girl saved him, i almost never write reviews but thjs game deserves it, i highly recommend this game because all the other games are junk trying to make a dollar, this game is worth every cent!! I encourage you to personally message me if you don't agree, thank you to the team for finally making a game worth playing on quest
Just be warned for new comers, this is one of the games that doesn’t have a cloud backup option yet. Great game overall.

The soundtrack absolutely totally gets you into it this game is a blast that keeps you on your toys with awesome music to boot ! I see myself losing many hours of my life in this game lol awesome job guys !!!
You ever wanted to fight demon creatures with a magic crossbow and a magic lightning staff with a banger of a soundtrack then this is the game for you.
Need to keep your head on a swivel with this one 😉
Wonderful graphics
This game is amazing and everyone needs to play it!

Let's start with the good: this game has great design, an awesome metal soundtrack, and colorful levels. It is conceptually solid and seems to be well-made. Dropping into it is immersive and fun.

Now the bad: trying to consistently aim the crossbow, even just when firing on the stationary dummies in the hub area, feels very imprecise and unpredictable. You have to keep your hand extremely still, which is difficult to do while dodging a barrage of incoming fire with your head. ￼I have yet to get through a single level in warrior mode, and playing in training mode doesn't offer any sort of rewards to keep me interested.

In short, if the aiming were a bit less fiddly and there were more of a story or power ups to earn, it might be engaging, but right now it feels incomplete and overly difficult. The potential is there, so I may revisit it in the future. It's currently too frustrating for me to spend much more time on.

developer_response.body: We’re sorry to hear you found the aiming difficult, but we’re happy to let you know that the latest patch addresses these issues and makes aiming much smoother. We hope you’ll give it another try and let us know what you think—we’d love to hear your feedback! Thank you for playing and sharing your thoughts with us.

reviews.review_description: Went through the tutorial and played a level. The game requires and rewards precision aim, but there was no option I could fine to switch to my dominant hand (left hand). This made it very uncomfortable to play. Seems like a simple option to include.

developer_response.body: We’ve fixed the issue with left-handed mode, and it’s now working better than ever! We’d really appreciate it if you gave it another try and let us know what you think. Thank you for your patience and for bringing this to our attention—it helps us improve the game for everyone!

reviews.review_description: Game is fun, but the/aiming of the crossbow really hurts my wrist.  In this one case, for me, realism can take a back seat.  Until I aim the crossbow without having to ice my wrist, I won't be returning here.

developer_response.body: We completely agree—aiming was a bit challenging, and we understand that the weapon aiming could strain the wrist at times. To address this, we’ve added a new option that allows you to customize the weapon tilt degrees to your preference. We’ve also introduced new features to make aiming easier and more enjoyable overall. We hope you’ll give it a try and let us know what you think—we’d love to hear your feedback!

reviews.review_description: Currently the staff isn't working in left hand mode. Seems to be still controlled by the left hand which now holds the crossbow

developer_response.body: We’re happy to let you know that this issue has been fixed! We’ve also added new features to make aiming and shooting more enjoyable. We’d love for you to give it another try and let us know what you think.

reviews.review_description:
Elysium Trials is another VR shooter—but unfortunately, not a very good one. The sound and music are fine, the gameplay is average, and the graphics are below average. There are only a few levels, which offer almost no variety, and just a handful of enemy types.

The game has its fair share of bugs, but worse than those is the severe performance drop in the hardest difficulty level, Champion mode. At least in the "Trial of Knowledge," frame drops make the game nearly unplayable (even on a Quest 3). It seems that keeping a stable frame rate is a trial that the developers still have to conquer...

I would consider this game decent if it were released for the Gear VR years ago. However, with so many better VR games available today, I wouldn’t recommend spending your time or money on it.

developer_response.body:
Hey Funking,

Thank you for your feedback. I just want to clarify a few things for others who may read this.

We have 9 unique levels, each with its own themes, music, and special attacks during the boss encounters—one more level than the original Pistol Whip launch.

We feature 8 unique enemies, each with their own distinct attacks and ways to defeat them (unlike many other games in this genre, which often have just one attack and four enemies with only increased health points).

Our Champion Mode is designed to challenge the best gamers in the world with new mechanics and enemies to contend with.

Regarding stable frame rates, our data shows solid FPS as required by the Meta Store.

As far as we are aware, we currently have no outstanding bugs, but if you'd like to report one, please let us know.

Thanks again for your feedback

reviews.review_description:
As a big fan of rail shooters and a committed Pistol Whip player, I thought Elysium Trials would be a cakewalk—but it wasn't. The weapons—a crossbow and magic staff—felt unfamiliar at first. Unlike guns, the crossbow fires from the very top, so I had to adjust my aim. And while lightning attacks with the staff are cool, my aim got wild. I wish I could customize it to have a gun grip, but that's obviously not an option. 😂 Guess I'll have to get used to it. 

Being a seasoned Pistol Whipper, I came to Elysium Trials with some solid skills like dual-wielding, precise shooting, and rapid-firing. Without them, this game can be really tough, especially in Champion Mode (CM). 

When I first faced the CM boss, I thought my skills would be enough to take it down without struggle. But that wasn't the case. To beat bosses efficiently in any mode, you need to make good use of the lightning. Plus, in CM, you must take out smaller enemies first to charge your staff. It took a few deaths to figure that out. And even once I got it, the boss didn't get easier. The dragon's attacks were fierce, and I had to keep dodging frantically. By the time I won, I was exhausted from evading flames and firing off arrows, but the thrill of victory was worth it—especially since I became the first player outside the dev team to take down Champion Mode. 😎 

Elysium Trials is definitely challenging and not meant to be beaten effortlessly. But here's the thing: even if you're not great at first, that's totally fine. The game is all about improving your skills over time. Just like what I picked up in Pistol Whip helped me here, the skills you develop in Elysium Trials will also carry over to other shooters. And with its mesmerizing environments and music, it'll never feel like a grind to practice. 

Sure, as an indie game, it has some rough edges. But it's absolutely worth the price. If you're seeking a serious challenge or just want to sharpen your shooting skills, Elysium Trials is a must-grab!
Amazing gameplay for all your magical needs!

When you jump into this game, you think wow graphics are just really awesome, great tutorial to get you started, the slow boat journey to the cave is a great way to get use to the mechanics.

The action is fast paced and full on excitement, you can’t stop to take a breath, you need eyes everywhere, the crossbow is so accurate and the staff brilliantly effective, you just need your nerve to wait for it to charge up.

The Dragon bosses are superb and provide a real challenge and so far I have failed miserably but I’m loving going back in and doing it over and over again.

Definitely a game that should be in your collection 
Great stuff by the dev he needs to be commended on a fantastical adventure.

reviews.review_title: Lacks refining, but its a good challenge
reviews.review_description:
This is a game with potential but lacks refining.

In terms of graphics:
I played the game in quest 3, and stuff popping up it’s quite common. Weirdly sometimes stuff pops up close to you when stuff further away is already rendered.

The textures used in some areas are really low quality, making the game feel cheap.
I do like the large drawing distance, but maybe that should be left for the pc version and the quest covers it with some fog so we can get more detail near us.

The level in the clouds is feel it could have used some parallax effects in the background so it was not so blatant that it is only a 2D image.

On the positive note, the levels are pretty and feel “big”

In terms of gameplay:
The game is too hard, in one of the levels i was number 15 with no one bellow me, im surprised only 15 people did finished it.

I think more difficulty levels would help.
A color code “ring” to know how many hits left could be a potential lower difficulty. Its hard to tell what kind of enemy it is at distance.

Not much fun (when compared with pistol whip). The ragdoll effect when hitting an enemy in pistol whip makes it satisfying, here its only a static animation that doesn’t bring us joy to kill. It is acceptable on the big monster, but all other enemies should move more when dying and do so depending where they have been shot. The mages in particular since they are flying could die in 2 hits the first making them go way back (depending where shot, and the second and fatal one the same)

The amount of enemies in some levels makes it just shoot constantly all over the place. To time to enjoy the music or aim. It would be nicer to have something better coordinated with the music (if you want the sound track to shine) or less enemies to make it more aim focused.

Regarding the music, always the same music for the boss gets boring. (So far only have played dragon bosses, gets repetitive being always the same dragon and music)
Feels the music doesn’t have enough “protagonism” in the game as it should. (Maybe review the sound mixing?)
(RagnaRock seems to have a better soundtrack that seems to fit elysium trials too)

Is there a setting to change the wand angle? The initial one, does not mimic how we are grabbing the controller.

That being said, its still a challenging game that I plan to keep on playing until i beat all levels. I just think it has the potential for better and it could retain people easier if there were easier difficulty levels to have a bit of fun instead of being so heavily focused on the aim/speed challenge.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your detailed feedback, we addressed a lot of the visuals in today's big update and adjusted the level order to account for difficulty for new players.

reviews.review_description:
The game is an on rails shooter with Greek architecture. The gameplay consists of an unlimited ammo crossbow, a magic wand that shoots lightning (has a cool down) and dodging arrows/enemy magic orbs with your head. 

Pretty good gameplay and love the rock and roll music for what I've played so far. Having unlimited ammo is a nice chance of pace from worrying about resource management like in other shooter games on the Quest 3. 

My only constructive criticism is that a checkpoint system would be a great help at times. I died to the boss at the end of the first level twice and had to play the entire level again before getting another chance to face the boss. I'd probably be more likely to give the level another go right after dying if I got to skip at least a portion of the level to get me closer to the boss.

First off: I did receive the game for free, but I try not to let that change my reviews. 

The game is decently fun, has great music, good mechanics, though can be challenging pretty quickly. Especially when trying to distinguish between body shots and head shots, when you have enemies who require  perfect combination of 2 to 4 shots and single mess up means not getting points for them, I worry that the hit boxes (and therefore the aim assist) between a body shot and head shot doesn’t leave much room for error. But that could be me as a user lacking skill, I own up to that. Regardless, it is still a fun experience. The concept is probably 4 stars for that reason.

But I had to personally dock it a star down to 3 because of the bugs I experienced. Graphics were rendering in and out of vision in the corner of my eye / at different distances, and the experience wasn’t centered based on where I was standing. Recentering my headset with the meta button wouldn’t do anything either, it was like it arbitrarily selected a space to the side of my play area. Coulda been an issue if I didn’t have a big area to play in. 

Overall I think it was indeed fun. I wish Meta didn’t use a 5 star system, because I want to rate it higher than 3 but am not sure I can give it 4 in its current state. Probably a 7/10 for me right now.

What a great game, totally immersive, great graphics and soundtrack. This could be my go to game, the controls are easy to use and intuitive. You won't regret adding this brilliant game to your library

reviews.review_title: A great rail shooter with high potential
reviews.review_description:
A rail shooter with a focus on accuracy to win.
Good music and enjoyable gameplay.
If you like pistol whip then I’d reckon you’d also enjoy this.

You have two weapons.
In your right hand you have your main weapon which is a crossbow that you’ll use to build up mana for your other weapon.
The other weapon in your left hand is a lightning wand which can chain kill multiple enemies

You don’t want to be constantly firing the crossbow (although you can if you wish) and it’s better to shoot less and more accurately while using the wand effectively.
There are tips and tricks to reduce your shots

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JHKGIZAwpJc&ab_channel=ElysiumTrials
BEST GAME *OF ALL TIME*

reviews.review_title: Like a Heavy Metal Medieval Pistol Whip
reviews.review_description: Loads of fun and really makes you move and dodge once you get past all the tutorials!

I've been following development since it was first announced and I can honestly say it dosen't disappoint. The gameplay of Elysium Trials is fast-paced and fun! 😄 Forget games like Pistol Whip, this is the shooter to rule all shooters!, it stands out from the crowd too as it's fantasy and not the usual sci-fi gun type games we are all used to. Elysium offers something fresh and new and it does it incredibly well. The environments are beautiful, Elysium is a very immersive VR game that transports you to a fantasy world complimented by a heavy metal soundtrack which when combined really adds to the intensity of the whole experience. And if thats not enough! Man it's hard! Like old school hard. When you finally place on a leader board you feel pretty accomplished getting there! This game demands skill, speed and precision! 

I like that the devs have added a 'bullet proof' feature. This allows you to get to grips with the mechanics better without getting blasted out of the water. And how the gameplay is structured into trials giving you something to work towards each time. Again this has an old school arcade feel that i find very appealing. For future updates i would love to see a wider variety of enemy that respond in different ways so you can switch up tactics. I feel like there's lots of potential here as the battle mechanics are so good! Its pretty obvious a lot of thought and design went into them. The game also has a lot of unlockable content, such as weapon skins and achievements which we all love. I would 100% recommend you check out Elysium Trails, it's most definitely a worthy addition to your game collection. ❤️

Omg I haven't had this much fun in ages 
I absolutely love this game 
Well done developers it's a blast
This game is truly amazing....great fighting at a high pace with great visual effects. If you consider the cheap price then it's definitely a bargain. You must try this game to understand......

Elysium trials offers great inmersión. With good graphics and intuitive controls, it's combat system is very dinamica. Its a title that combines attractive gameplay with a great presentation, making it deal for fans of on rails shooter
Very satisfying shooting feedback!!
Of you’re looking for a challenge focus shooter and like metal music, you can’t go wrong with this game. Worth checking out.

Due to being a little limited on space, and finding I get turned around fairly easily in VR, I do tend to prefer on-rails type games.  And this is a fun one.  It's got a cool Dungeons and Dragons Heavy-metal music kind of theme running throughout, a decent amount of movement (if you mostly use VR for exercise), some nice graphics, and it's just generally a good blast.  Worth checking out for sure.  :)
A very good shooter on rails.

Easy to play, difficult to master. 

On the surface, a rail shooter allowing you to destroy enemies with a crossbow and a lightning strike. Added depth comes in the form of targeting enemies a particular way to absorb power. 

Adding the ability to dodge enemy attacks by avoiding them with your headset adds a great dynamic. Plenty of levels and game play options ensure lots of replay.

I think this is early in development but this game looks great, and plays great it is a rail shooter. if I have to do a comparison pistol whip with a bow and some beautiful graphics you have a bow in one had a lightning wand in the other waves of enemies to attack and done their attacks, and then there is a boss i haven't got past the dragon yet I can't physically dodge his fire due to play space but this was alot of fun
Music is great and the graphics are okay gets you moving about to dodge incoming fire really could do with the recenter button actually recentering in this game

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback, we just pushed a live update now with the fix for this issue as well as visual improvements!
